---

### Post by -synapse- on 2004-11-03
You have to add this line to the device section of the graphics card.
```
Option "UseInternalAGPGART" "no"
``` 

thanks

synapse

---

### Post by Slovak on 2004-11-03
OK, when I add that and reboot, if it boots to a black screen how do I recover?

---

### Post by -synapse- on 2004-11-03
[QUOTE=Slovak]OK, when I add that and reboot, if it boots to a black screen how do I recover?[/QUOTE]
 Change where in the device section it has the driver as:
```
 Driver "ati"
```
to
```
 Driver "fglrx"
``` 

and it will work.

Synapse

---

### Post by Slovak on 2004-11-03
It don't work, whether I say yes or no to useinternalagpgart, use ATI or fglrx for the driver, manual edit the config or run fglrxconfig. Any ideas?

---

### Post by -synapse- on 2004-11-03
[QUOTE=Slovak]It don't work, whether I say yes or no to useinternalagpgart, use ATI or fglrx for the driver, manual edit the config or run fglrxconfig. Any ideas?[/QUOTE]
 Well, as soon as I did apt-get install fglrx-driver and edited the config it worked perfect;y, cant imagine what is going wrong there m8, sorry

Yours says: 
> [B][COLOR=RED]
OpenGL vendor string: Mesa project: [www.mesa3d.org](www.mesa3d.org)
OpenGL renderer string: Mesa GLX Indirect
[SIZE=4]OpenGL version string: 1.3 Mesa 4.0.4[/SIZE][/COLOR][/B]

Did you by any chance install any of the SPM mesa files like [COLOR=BLUE]mesag3, mesag3+ggi, mesag3+ggi-dev, mesag3-glide2[/COLOR]?

Otherwise Ubuntu must be downloading different flgrx drivers according to mobo or kernel. Do you have an AMD motherboard? What kernel are you using?

"**OpenGL version string: 1.3 Mesa 4.0.4**" usually comes up when one downloads ATI's web drivers in .rpm format. ATI is now up to 3.14.1 while Debian is up to 3.12.0 on XFree86 version 4.3.0. 

Did you install ATI's web drivers before running SPM???

Do you have [COLOR=BLUE]libglib2.0.0 (installed version 2.4.7-0ubuntu2)[/COLOR] installed?


.

### Post by wallijonn on 2004-11-07
try using linux-image-2.6.8.1-3-686 (from Restricted Repository) and
linux-restricted-modules-2.6.8.1-3-686 (Description: non-free)

I installed those 686 kernels and they work with fglrx - DRI is enabled.

### Post by mattyh on 2004-11-07
I had the same problems you had with other versions of Linux.  ATI's installer is a little finicky, but I will say that it worked in about 5 minutes on Ubuntu.  I followed the instructions I found on the site, ran fglrxconfig, accepted all the defaults except I used an external AGPGart, and the next question about locked pages I selected the opposite of the default I think...anyways it worked right off.  ATI is supposed to be working on their drivers, so they say, so hopefully in the near future they'll be less of a headache.

---

### Post by Slovak on 2004-11-07
I understand what you are saying, but hell, when I even try gentoo with their excellent portage system and all one has to do is emerge the drivers, and I get the same results I think it is time to give up  on linux. If one can't get ATI working with gentoo and the easiest way in linux to get software up and running, well then I don't know what else to say.
Right now I am using MDK10.1 and it all works flawlessly, except of course for ATI 3D, even my 5 button mouse works as if it were in windows, no sound problems either, plus the big plus for me is KDE because I hate gnome, so until someone can get my ATI working for me I think I'll stay right here. My sound was screwy in Ubuntu too, worked at times, and sometimes it didn't. Don't get me wrong, Ubuntu is a fine distro, but gnome isn't for me, and besides, what difference does it make which distro one uses if they only have 2D?

---

### Post by mattyh on 2004-11-07
I just wrote a HOWTO over in the HOWTO section, feel free to give that a try if you like.  I know how you feel, I've been using Linux off and on for 2 years now.  But, for the most part it didn't stay on my system past a week or two at most.  Not because I didn't enjoy it, but because there are certain things I want to be able to do and sometimes it just doesn't feel like it is there.  I will say though, for me, Ubuntu is the first one where it really feels "there", so I'll be sticking with it.  I oftentimes give up until the next release cycle and then try again there.  You might want to try that.

I know that you've given up - I am just curious.

### Post by Tiraid on 2005-04-27
This may be too little too late, but I don't know where else to post it, and this sounds similar.

I just got fglrx working in hoary after hours of trying every howto I could read. The funny thing is, it worked the first time, but not at 1600x1200. A coworker came in, looked at it my problem for about a second, then hit ctrl+alt+-, and changed my resolution. 1280x1024 works fine. My only problem was the screen resolution.

The os ati driver can handle 1600x1200, but fglrx cannot. Anyone know why this may be?
